import { Meta, Title, Canvas } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks' import { FigmaLink } from '../../storybook/components' import * as IconsStories from './Icons.stories.tsx' Icons Burrito **Icons** are sourced from FontAwesome v6 and are all available as separate React components. ## Usage ### Basic Icons are available through a separate import to provide a better experience with IntelliSense. { return ( ) }` }}/> ### With `` To align an icon with some accompanying text, use the [**Text** component](https://burrito-design-system.jane.qa/?path=/docs/components-typography-text--docs) and simply include the icon component as the first immediate descendent. ## Static assets All icons are also provided in SVG format if you have a specific need that React components do not meet. **Note:** This approach will require you to configure your bundler to handle SVG files. We recommend using [SVGR](https://react-svgr.com/docs/getting-started/). ```tsx import Lock from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system/assets/icons/lock.svg' import Star from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system/assets/icons/star.svg' export const MyComponent = () => { return (
) } ``` ## Accessibility There are two different approaches to make icons accessible based on their usage: When the icon is used on its own and when it's used for decorative purposes accompanied by a text equivalent. ### Icon only When a icon is used on its own, it holds a specific meaning that needs to be accessible by all users. In most cases the icon will be used in a focusable parent (link, button, form input...). To meet a11y standards we recommend to use `aria-hidden` on the icon and an `aria-label` on the parent to provide context. In cases where the parent is non focusable we recommend to use an `aria-label` on the icon itself. ```tsx import { Phone, Lock } from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system/icons' export const MyComponent = () => ( <>
) ``` ### Icon with text If the icon is used alongside a text equivalent, the associated text provides this context. In this case where the icon is used for decorative purposes, apply the `aria-hidden` attribute to prevent assistive technologies from announcing the presence of the icon. ```tsx import { Phone } from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system/icons' export const MyComponent = () => ( <> Contact Us ) ``` ## API ### Props The icon components have all the props present on a [SVGSVGElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/SVGSVGElement) except for the following: `fill`, `focusable`, `role`, `viewBox`, `xmlns`. ## Reference Below are all the available icons with their component name. ## Design Iconography page in UI Kit

import { Meta, Title, Canvas, Controls } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'
import { FigmaLink } from '../../storybook/components'
import * as LinkStories from './Link.stories.tsx'

<Meta of={LinkStories} />

<Title>Link</Title>

**Links** are used for navigation and usually appear as part of a sentence.

## Usage

### Default

<Canvas of={LinkStories.Default} sourceState="shown" source={{
  code: `
  import { Link } from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system'

export const MyComponent = () => {
  return (
    <Link href="#top" target="_self">
      Link to current page
    </Link>
  )
}`}}  />

### Inline

<Canvas of={LinkStories.Inline} />

### Standalone

To force the link to render on its own line without wrapping in a `div` or `p`
element, use the `isStandalone` prop.

<Canvas of={LinkStories.Standalone} />

## API

### Props

The Link component has all the props present on a
[HTMLAnchorElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLAnchorElement)
plus the following:

| Prop               | Type      | required | default | Description                       |
| ------------------ | --------- | -------- | ------- | --------------------------------- |
| **`isStandalone`** | `boolean` | No       | `false` | Renders the link on its own line. |

## Design

<FigmaLink href="https://www.figma.com/design/pDDlKkiRPt9NA2JdA6eyeY/%F0%9F%8C%AF-Burrito-Design-System-UI-Kit?node-id=6060-5762">
  Link page in UI Kit
</FigmaLink>

> **Note**: In Figma, there is no **Link** component, instead underlined "Text
> styles" are applied to text.

components-menu--docs.content:
import { Meta, Title, Canvas, Controls } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'
import { Design } from '@storybook/addon-designs/blocks'
import { A11yTestStatus, FigmaLink } from '../../storybook/components'
import * as MenuStories from './Menu.stories.tsx'
import { sourceTransform } from './Menu.stories.utils.ts'

<Meta of={MenuStories} />

<Title>Menu</Title>

A **Menu** is a list of actions or navigation links activated from a button or
trigger element. It differs from a [Select](?path=/docs/components-form-inputs-select--docs)
input in that it shows actions or navigation options and is not a form input.

<Canvas of={MenuStories.Default} source={{ transform: sourceTransform }} />
<Controls of={MenuStories.Default} />

## Usage

### Basic Usage

For the `Menu` component to work it needs to be used with `useMenu` and an element to attach the menu to.
It also needs to be used with its sub-components which are collection components.
[Learn how it works under the hood](#how-it-works).

Each item component (`Menu.Item`, `Menu.DestructiveItem` and `Menu.LinkItem`)
used inside the `Menu` must have a `key` prop with a unique identifier.

When the user selects an item, the `onAction` callback function provided to the `Menu` component is called with
the `key` of the selected item.

<Canvas
  of={MenuStories.Basic}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
import { Menu } from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system'\n

const MyApp = () => {
const { menuProps, triggerProps } = useMenu()
return (

<>
  <Button {...triggerProps} isMenuButton>
    Open Menu
  </Button>
  <Menu {...menuProps} onAction={(selectedKey) => {}}>
    <Menu.Item key="item" aria-label="Item">
      <Star aria-hidden />
      Item
    </Menu.Item>
  </Menu>
</>
) }` }} />

### Groups

You can use the `Menu.Group` sub-component to group together items that belong
together. You can also use its `title` prop to include a visible label for the
group.

<Canvas
  of={MenuStories.Groups}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
<Menu>
  <Menu.Group>
    <Menu.Item key="1.a">Item 1.A</Menu.Item>
    <Menu.Item key="1.b">Item 1.B</Menu.Item>
  </Menu.Group>
  <Menu.Group title="Group 2">
    <Menu.Item key="2.a">Item 1.A</Menu.Item>
    <Menu.Item key="2.b">Item 1.B</Menu.Item>
  </Menu.Group>
</Menu>`,
  }}
/>

### Right aligned

By default, the `Menu` will be horizontally aligned with the left edge of its
trigger element. If your menu needs to be displayed on the right edge of the
trigger, you can use the `position` prop to align it with the right edge.

<Canvas
  of={MenuStories.RightAligned}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
<Menu position="right">
  {/* Menu content */}
</Menu>
`,
  }}
/>

### Custom z-index

If the `Menu` is hidden behind another element it means that you will need
to adjust the `z-index` value. To achieve this you can use the custom `zIndex`
prop.

```jsx
<Menu zIndex={1000}>{/* Menu content */}</Menu>
```

### Icon

You can add a leading icon to your menu items to add more visual cues.

<Canvas
  of={MenuStories.ItemWithIcon}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
<Menu.Item key="item" aria-label="Item">
  <Star aria-hidden />
  Item
</Menu.Item>
`,
  }}
/>

### Badge

It is also possible to add a [Badge](?path=/docs/components-badge--docs) to
menu items.

<Canvas
  of={MenuStories.ItemWithBadge}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
<Menu.Item key="item" aria-label="Item">
  Item
  <Badge tone="discovery">New</Badge>
</Menu.Item>
`,
  }}
/>

### Links

Menu items can also be links, both internal and external

<Canvas
  of={MenuStories.LinkItem}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
<Menu.LinkItem key="link-a" href="#" aria-label="Link to page">
  Link to page
</Menu.LinkItem>
<Menu.LinkItem
  key="external-link-a"
  href="https://burrito-design-system.jane.qa"
  isExternal
  aria-label="Burrito DS website"
>
  Burrito DS website
</Menu.LinkItem>
`,
  }}
/>

### Destructive items

For items that will specifically trigger a "destructive" action, you can use the
`Menu.DestructiveAction` subcomponent.

If you ever need to use a different icon than the "trash" icon you can also use
the `isCritical` prop on `Menu.Item`.

<Canvas
  of={MenuStories.DestructiveItem}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
<Menu.DestructiveItem key="delete">
  Delete
</Menu.DestructiveItem>
`,
  }}
/>

### Disabled items

You can disable items by using the `disabledKeys` prop. It's an array of item keys representing the disabled items.

<Canvas
  of={MenuStories.DisabledItem}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
<Menu disabledKeys={['a']}>
  <Menu.Item key="a">Item A</Menu.Item>
  <Menu.Item key="b">Item B</Menu.Item>
</Menu>
`,
  }}
/>

### Loading State

The `isLoading` prop can be used to display a loading state. It will replace the
content of each item with a `SkeletonBar` component and add `aria-busy` on the
`Menu`.

<Canvas
  of={MenuStories.Loading}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
<Menu isLoading>
  <Menu.Item key="1.a">Item 1.A</Menu.Item>
  <Menu.Item key="1.b">Item 1.B</Menu.Item>
</Menu>
`,
  }}
/>

### Menu triggers

The design specs specify three type of buttons that can trigger a Menu: button,
icon button and "split button". You make those triggers by using the
`isMenuButton` prop on [Button](?path=/docs/components-button--docs) and
[IconButton](?path=/docs/components-iconbutton--docs) as well as the
[ButtonGroup](?path=/docs/components-buttongroup--docs) component.

<Canvas of={MenuStories.DifferentTriggers}
        sourceState="shown"
        source={{
          code: `
{/* button */}
<Button isMenuButton {...triggerProps}>
  Open Menu
</Button>

{/* icon button */}

<IconButton {...triggerProps} isMenuButton aria-label="open icon button menu">
  <Ellipsis aria-hidden />
</IconButton>

{/* Split button */}

<ButtonGroup isAlwaysHorizontal>
  <Button>Button</Button>
  <IconButton {...splitButtonTriggerProps} isMenuButton aria-label="open split button menu">
    <Ellipsis aria-hidden />
  </IconButton>
</ButtonGroup>
`}} />

## Accessibility

### Use `aria-label` or `textValue` mixed Item content

The `Menu` component comes with a typeahead feature that is useful for keyboard
navigation. By typing the the first letters of an item, the selection will
automatically jump to the first matching item.

The typeahead feature works automatically when an item content is only text. But
if you add an icon or a Badge you will need to set an `aria-label` value. If
`aria-label` is already set to a different value for accessibility reason, you
can use `textValue` instead

<Canvas
  of={MenuStories.Typeahead}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
const { menuProps, triggerProps } = useMenu()
return (<>
  <Button isMenuButton {...triggerProps}>
    Open Menu
  </Button>
  <Menu {...menuProps} onAction={args.onAction}>
    <Menu.Item key="dollar" aria-label="dollar">
      <Dollar aria-hidden />
      Dollar
    </Menu.Item>
    <Menu.Item key="download" aria-label="download">
      <Download aria-hidden />
      Download
    </Menu.Item>
    <Menu.Item key="bold" aria-label="Change font-weight to bold" textValue="Bold">
      <Bold aria-hidden />
      Bold
    </Menu.Item>
  </Menu>
</>
)`,
  }}
/>

### Accessibility test status

#### General tests

| Test                                                                                                                                                                | WCAG Level                                                                                            | Status                                           |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| **Menu works on mobile devices.** On mobile devices and tablets, Menu should work in both portrait and landscape orientation with no loss of functionality.         | **AA** ([1.3.4 Orientation](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/orientation))                 | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest /> |
| **Color is not the only method used to convey meaning.** When viewing a Menu, you never have to rely only on color to understand content.                           | **A** ([1.4.1 Use of color](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/use-of-color))                | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest /> |
| **Text content inside the Menu has proper contrast.** When viewing the Menu, the text content within it has 4.5:1 contrast for both selected and deselected states. | **AA** ([1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum)](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/contrast-minimum)) ]   | <A11yTestStatus status="success" />              |
| **Menu icon elements have proper contrast.** The contrast between the icons of the menu items is at least 3:1 from their respective surrounding backgrounds.        | **AA** ([1.4.11 Non-text contrast](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/non-text-contrast))    | <A11yTestStatus status="success" />              |
| **Menu has appropriate labels and instructions.** When you view or interact with the Menu component on your page, it is accompanied by a descriptive label.         | **AA** ([2.4.6 Headings and labels](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/headings-and-labels)) | <A11yTestStatus status="conditional" />          |

#### Keyboard navigation tests

| Test                                                                                                                                                                                      | WCAG Level                                                                                | Status                                           |
| ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| **Focus indicator is clearly visible around Menu.** When you tab into a Menu, a visible outline or other clear indication of focus appears around the Menu and items within.              | **AA** ([2.4.7 Focus visible](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/focus-visible)) | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest /> |
| **Tabbing into the Menu doesn’t trigger a change of context.** When using a keyboard to access the Menu, there is no change until you activate with <kbd>Enter</kbd> or <kbd>Space</kbd>. | **A** ([3.2.1 On Focus](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/on-focus))            | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest /> |
| **Keyboard actions can access all Menu functionality.** You can type, select, and perform all other functions in a Menu element using only your keyboard.                                 | **A** ([2.1.1 Keyboard](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/keyboard))            | <A11yTestStatus status="success" />              |

#### Screen reader tests

| Test                                                                                                                                                                                           | WCAG Level                                                                                                        | Status                                           |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| **Screen reader announces type and number of options.** When interacting with a menu group with a screen reader, you hear how many options there are.                                          | **A** ([1.3.1 Info and relationships](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/info-and-relationships))        | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest /> |
| **Screen reader announces “menu” upon initial interaction.** When you tab onto a menu with a screen reader, you hear “menu”.                                                                   | **AA** ([1.3.5 Identify input purpose](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/identify-purpose))             | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest /> |
| **Screen readers identify menus the same way when more than one is on a page.** When you tab to a menu on a page that has other menues, you will hear “menu” every time you tab to a new menu. | **AA** ([3.2.4 Consistent identification](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/consistent-identification)) | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest /> |

## API

### Props

The Menu component has all the props present on
a [HTMLDivElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLDivElement) plus the following:

| Prop               | Type                    | required | default | Description                                                                                                        |
| ------------------ | ----------------------- | -------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **`onAction`**     | `(key: string) => void` | Yes      | N/A     | Callback function called when an item has been selected. The `key` parameter matches the key of the selected item. |
| **`disabledKeys`** | `string[]`              | No       | N/A     | The keys of items that are disabled.                                                                               |
| **`isLoading`**    | `boolean`               | No       | `false` | Flag used to enable the loading view of the Menu                                                                   |
| **`position`**     | `left \| right`         | No       | `left`  | Horizontal alignment of the Menu relative to its trigger.                                                          |
| **`zIndex`**       | `number`                | No       | `110`   | The CSS `z-index` value for the Menu                                                                               |
| **`state`**        | `object`                | Yes      | N/A     | Menu state provided by [useMenu](#useMenu).                                                                        |
| **`triggerRef`**   | `ref`                   | Yes      | N/A     | The `ref` of the element that the menu is attached to. Provided by [useMenu](#useMenu).                            |

#### Menu.Item props

The `Menu.Item` is a collection component provided by React Aria. It is the
default item component for the Menu.

| Prop             | Type               | required    | default | Description                                                                                                                           |
| ---------------- | ------------------ | ----------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`key`**        | `string \| number` | Yes         | N/A     | Unique identifier for the item. It is required for use with the `onAction` callback and to enable keyboard navigation.                |
| **`aria-label`** | `string`           | Conditional | N/A     | Accessible label for the item. This prop is also required to enable typeahead when the item contains non text item (Icon or badge).   |
| **`textValue`**  | `string`           | No          | N/A     | Use this prop if you want to use a different value for the typeahead feature than what is used for `aria-label`                       |
| **`isCritical`** | `boolean`          | No          | `false` | Use this feature to enable the critical color theming on the item. See also [Menu.DestructiveItem props](#menu-destructiveitem-props) |

#### Menu.DestructiveItem props

The `Menu.DestructiveItem` is a collection component provided by React Aria. It comes
by default with the critical color theming and has the [Trash icon](?path=/story/components-icons--reference)
as the leading icon.

| Prop             | Type               | required    | default | Description                                                                                                                         |
| ---------------- | ------------------ | ----------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`key`**        | `string \| number` | Yes         | N/A     | Unique identifier for the item. It is required to enable keyboard navigation.                                                       |
| **`aria-label`** | `string`           | Conditional | N/A     | Accessible label for the item. This prop is also required to enable typeahead when the item contains non text item (Icon or badge). |
| **`textValue`**  | `string`           | No          | N/A     | Use this prop if you want to use a different value for the typeahead feature than what is used for `aria-label`                     |

#### Menu.LinkItem props

The `Menu.LinkItem` is a collection component provided by React Aria. It will be
rendered as an anchor and as such reuse some of the native anchor features (see
[MDN](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Reference/Elements/a)):
`href`, `hrefLang`, `target`, `rel`, `download`, `ping`, `referrerPolicy`

| Prop                | Type               | required    | default | Description                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------------- | ------------------ | ----------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **`key`**           | `string \| number` | Yes         | N/A     | Unique identifier for the item. It is required to enable keyboard navigation.                                                                                      |
| **`aria-label`**    | `string`           | Conditional | N/A     | Accessible label for the item. This prop is also required to enable typeahead when the item contains non text item (Icon or badge).                                |
| **`isExternal`**    | `boolean`          | No          | `false` | Accessible label for the item. This prop is also required to enable typeahead when the item contains non text item (Icon or badge).                                |
| **`textValue`**     | `string`           | No          | N/A     | Use this prop if you want to use a different value for the typeahead feature than what is used for `aria-label`                                                    |
| **`routerOptions`** | `object`           | No          | N/A     | This prop is used to integrate with various client side routing. See [Client Side Routing](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/routing.html) on React Aria |

#### Menu.Section props

| Prop        | Type     | required | default | Description                                                                      |
| ----------- | -------- | -------- | ------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`title`** | `string` | No       | N/A     | Content of the Section header. Use when you want to set a header on your section |

### Custom hooks

#### useMenu

The `useMenu` hook is used to power the visibility mechanism between the `Menu`
and its target element.

See the [Usage](#usage) section to learn how to use this hook.

##### Arguments

`useMenu` takes an object as argument. This object can have the following
fields:

- `defaultIsOpen`: sets the default visibility of the component. `false` by default
- `triggerRef`: Optional field that you can use in case you have need to pass your own `ref` to the trigger element

##### Return value

- `triggerProps`: props to be passed to the element that open the `Menu`, typically a button.
- `menuProps`: props to be passed to the `Menu` component.

## How it works

This section is here purely to give you context on how the `Menu` component
was built. These implementation details will not impact you directly, but you
may be surprised by what you see if you use the
[React Developer Tools](https://react.dev/learn/react-developer-tools) on a
`Menu`. The following explanation will help you understand how the
component actually works.

The `Menu` component is built on top of React Aria's [useMenu](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-aria/useMenu.html)
and React Stately's [useTreeState](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-stately/useTreeState.html).
These hooks require the use of React Stately's components `Item` and `Section`
which are [collection components](https://react-spectrum.adobe.com/react-stately/collections.html).

To understand more about how collection components work see Data Table
["How it works"](?path=/docs/components-datatable--docs#how-it-works) section.

## Design

<FigmaLink href="https://www.figma.com/design/pDDlKkiRPt9NA2JdA6eyeY/%F0%9F%8C%AF-Burrito-Design-System-UI-Kit?node-id=17380-110">
  Menu component in UI Kit
</FigmaLink>

### UX guidelines

<Design of={MenuStories.Default} />

stories.snippet:
const Default = ({ isDismissDisabled, size }) => {
  const { state, triggerProps, modalProps } = useModal()
  return (
    <>
      <Button {...triggerProps}>Open Modal</Button>
      <Modal size={size} state={state} isDismissDisabled={isDismissDisabled} {...modalProps}>
        <Modal.Header>
          <Modal.Title>Modal Title</Modal.Title>
          <Modal.CloseButton onClick={state.close} />
        </Modal.Header>
        <Modal.Content>
          <Text style={{ marginTop: 0 }}>
            This is a content slot. Create a local component of your content and swap it out using the Content
            property in the Figma sidebar.
          </Text>
          <Text style={{ marginBottom: 0 }}>
            I love cheese, especially camembert de normandie danish fontina. Roquefort cut the cheese mozzarella say
            cheese taleggio stinking bishop halloumi parmesan. Mascarpone smelly cheese when the cheese comes out
            everybody's happy cheesy grin danish fontina squirty cheese camembert de normandie cheese strings. St.
            agur blue cheese.
          </Text>
        </Modal.Content>
        <Modal.Footer>
          <Button onClick={state.close}>Cancel</Button>
          <PrimaryButton onClick={state.close}>Confirm</PrimaryButton>
        </Modal.Footer>
      </Modal>
    </>
  )
};

stories.snippet:
const Sizes = () => {
  const { state, triggerProps, modalProps } = useModal()
  const { state: otherState, triggerProps: otherTriggerProps, modalProps: otherModalProps } = useModal()
  return (
    <>
      <Button {...triggerProps}>Open Medium Modal</Button>

      <Modal state={state} {...modalProps} size="md">
        <Modal.Header>
          <Modal.Title>Modal Title</Modal.Title>
          <Modal.CloseButton onClick={state.close} />
        </Modal.Header>
        <Modal.Content>
          <Text style={{ margin: 0 }}>
            This is a content slot. Create a local component of your content and swap it out using the Content
            property in the Figma sidebar.
          </Text>
        </Modal.Content>
        <Modal.Footer>
          <Button onClick={state.close}>Cancel</Button>
          <PrimaryButton onClick={state.close}>Confirm</PrimaryButton>
        </Modal.Footer>
      </Modal>

      <Button {...otherTriggerProps}>Open Large Modal</Button>

      {otherState.isOpen && (
        <Modal state={otherState} {...otherModalProps} size="lg">
          <Modal.Header>
            <Modal.Title>Modal Title</Modal.Title>
            <Modal.CloseButton onClick={otherState.close} />
          </Modal.Header>
          <Modal.Content isContentCentered>
            <Text style={{ margin: 0 }}>
              This is a content slot. Create a local component of your content and swap it out using the Content
              property in the Figma sidebar.
            </Text>
          </Modal.Content>
          <Modal.Footer>
            <Button onClick={otherState.close}>Cancel</Button>
            <PrimaryButton onClick={otherState.close}>Confirm</PrimaryButton>
          </Modal.Footer>
        </Modal>
      )}
    </>
  )
};

const WithLongTitle = () => {
  const { state, triggerProps, modalProps } = useModal()
  const { state: otherState, triggerProps: otherTriggerProps, modalProps: otherModalProps } = useModal()
  return (
    <>
      <Button {...triggerProps}>Open Modal</Button>

      <Modal state={state} {...modalProps}>
        <Modal.Header>
          <Modal.Title>
            Modal heading that are long should be avoided but if it happens it will flow to the next line
          </Modal.Title>
          <Modal.CloseButton onClick={state.close} />
        </Modal.Header>
        <Modal.Content>
          <Text style={{ marginTop: 0 }}>
            This is a content slot. Create a local component of your content and swap it out using the Content
            property in the Figma sidebar.
          </Text>
        </Modal.Content>
        <Modal.Footer>
          <Button onClick={state.close}>Cancel</Button>
          <PrimaryButton onClick={state.close}>Confirm</PrimaryButton>
        </Modal.Footer>
      </Modal>

      <Button {...otherTriggerProps}>Open Modal without header and footer</Button>

      <Modal state={otherState} {...otherModalProps}>
        <Modal.Content isContentCentered>
          <Modal.Title>
            Modal heading that are long should be avoided but if it happens it will flow to the next line
          </Modal.Title>
          <Text style={{ margin: 0 }}>
            This is a content slot. Create a local component of your content and swap it out using the Content

components-modal--docs.name: Docs
components-modal--docs.title: Components/Modal
components-modal--docs.content:
import { Meta, Title, Canvas, Controls } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'
import { Design } from '@storybook/addon-designs/blocks'
import { A11yTestStatus, FigmaLink } from '../../storybook/components'
import * as ModalStories from './Modal.stories.tsx'

<Meta of={ModalStories} />

<Title>Modal</Title>

**Modals** are used to capture attention or request user input for specific
tasks, decisions, or actions.

<Canvas of={ModalStories.Default} />
<Controls of={ModalStories.Default} />

## Usage

For the `<Modal>` component to work, it needs to be used at least with
`useModal`, and `Modal.Content`.

### Default Usage

You can use the other `<Modal>` subcomponents to create your Modal:

<Canvas
  of={ModalStories.Default}
  sourceState="shown"
  source={{
    code: `
import { useModal, Modal, Button } from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system'\n
export const MyComponent = () => {
  const { state, triggerProps, modalProps } = useModal()
  return (
    <>
      <Button {...triggerProps}>OpenModal</Button>
      <Modal state={state} {...modalProps}>
        <Modal.Header>
          <Modal.Title>Modal Title</Modal.Title>
          <Modal.CloseButton onClick={state.close} />
        </Modal.Header>
        <Modal.Content>Modal Content</Modal.Content>
        <Modal.Footer>
          <Button onClick={state.close}>Cancel</Button>
          <PrimaryButton onClick={state.close}>Confirm</PrimaryButton>
        </Modal.Footer>
      </Modal>
    </>
  )
}`,
  }}
/>

### Sizes

The `<Modal>` component comes in two sizes: medium and large. You can change it
with the `size` prop and its default size is medium.

<Canvas of={ModalStories.Sizes} />

### Disable dismiss behaviour

If an action is required on the Modal, The user should be prevented from
closing it. You do so by using the `isDismissDisabled` prop on the
`<Modal>` component. This will prevent the modal to be closed if the
user clicks outside the modal or press the `Esc` key.

<Canvas of={ModalStories.Undismissable} />

### Without Header and Footer

It is possible to use the `Modal` without a header and footer. When that
happens, the design indicates that content of `Modal.Content` is centered.
You can achieve this by using the `isContentCentered` prop.

```tsx
import { useModal, Modal, Button } from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system'

export const MyComponent = () => {
  const { state, triggerProps, modalProps } = useModal()
  return (
    <>
      <Button {...triggerProps}>OpenModal</Button>
      <Modal state={state} {...modalProps}>
        <Modal.Content isContentCentered>
          <Modal.Title>Modal Title</Modal.Title>
          Modal Content
          <Button onClick={state.close}>Button</Button>
        </Modal.Content>
      </Modal>
    </>
  )
}
```

<Canvas of={ModalStories.WithoutHeaderAndFooter} />

### Custom z-index

If the `Modal` is hidden behind another element it means that you will need
to adjust the `z-index` value. To achieve this you can use the custom `zIndex`
prop.

<Canvas of={ModalStories.CustomZIndex} />

## Accessibility

### Ensure the `Modal` is labelled

The `Modal` component needs to have an accessible label. The `Modal.Title` is
implemented to be automatically used as a label. It is meant to be used in
`Modal.Header` or as the first element of `Modal.Content` for the version
without header.

If you're not able to use `Modal.Title` be sure to use the `aria-label` prop on
the `Modal` component.

```
<Modal aria-label="Informative modal">
  <Modal.Content>
    A success Modal provides a positive outcome or confirmation.
  </Modal.Content>
</Modal>
```

### Share more context with `aria-describedBy`

You may also use `aria-describedby` on the `<Modal>` component to associate
descriptive text to the modal window so that it’s read when opening the modal.
The `id` should belong to a paragraph or a brief piece of content.

```tsx
import { Modal, Heading } from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system'

export const App = () => {
  return (
    <>
      <Heading level={1}>Page heading</Heading>
      <Heading level={2}>Page sub-heading</Heading>
      <Modal aria-describedby="modal-description">
        <Modal.Content isContentCentered>
          <Modal.Title aria-level={3}>Modal title</Modal.Title>
          <Text id="modal-description">A success Modal provides a positive outcome or confirmation.</Text>
        </Modal.Content>
      </Modal>
    </>
  )
}
```

### Setting a different level to Modal.Title

The `Modal.Title` component uses the [Heading](?path=/docs/components-typography-heading--docs)
component with `level={2}` under the hood. You can use its `aria-level` prop to make sure your page
follows a consistent heading structure

```
<Modal>
  <Modal.Content>
    <Modal.Title aria-level={3}>Modal title</Modal.Title>
    A success Modal provides a positive outcome or confirmation.
  </Modal.Content>
</Modal>
```

### Accessibility tests status

#### General tests

| Test                                                                                                          | WCAG Level                                                                                                | Status                                  |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| All headings and labels are clear and descriptive. When you view the alert, the heading is clear and concise. | **AA** ([2.4.6 Heading and Labels](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/headings-and-labels.html)) | <A11yTestStatus status="conditional" /> |

#### Keyboard navigation tests

| Test                                                                                                                                       | WCAG Level                                                                                     | Status                                          |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| Modal can be accessed with only a keyboard. When you the modal displays, the focus shifts to it.                                           | **A** ([2.1.1 Keyboard](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/keyboard.html))            | <A11yTestStatus status="success"/>              |
| Focus indicators have proper size, perimeter, and contrast. When you use a keyboard to tab through the modal, a focus indicator is present | **AA** ([2.4.7 Focus Visible](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/focus-visible.html)) | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest/> |

#### Screen reader tests

| Test                                     | WCAG Level                                                                                                 | Status                                          |
| ---------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| Screen reader announces Modal content    | **A** ([1.3.1 Info and relationships](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/info-and-relationships)) | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest/> |
| Modal content is read in a logical order | **A** ([1.3.2 Meaningful sequence](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/meaningful-sequence))       | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest/> |

## API

### Props

The Modal component has all the props present on
a [HTMLDivElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLDivElement) plus the following:

| Prop                    | Type      | required | default | Description                                                                                                                |
| ----------------------- | --------- | -------- | ------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **`size`**              | `string`  | No       | `md`    | The size of the Modal. It will influence the component width.                                                              |
| **`isDismissDisabled`** | `boolean` | No       | `false` | Use this property to prevent the modal to be dismissed when the user clicks outside the modal or the `Esc` key is pressed. |
| **`state`**             | `object`  | Yes      | N/A     | This objects defines and controls the visibility state of the `Modal`. It is returned by the [useModal](#useModal) hook    |
| **`zIndex`**            | `number`  | No       | 999     | The CSS `z-index` value for the Modal's overlay and container.                                                             |

#### Modal.Title props

The `<Modal.Title>` itself does not define any unique props, but any valid props of an
[Heading](?path=/docs/components-typography-heading--docs) component (minus the `level` prop)
or a React `ref` will be passed onto the element that it renders.

#### Modal.Content props

The `<Modal.Content>` has all the props present on
a [HTMLDivElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLDivElement) plus the following:

| Prop                    | Type      | required | default | Description                                         |
| ----------------------- | --------- | -------- | ------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| **`isContentCentered`** | `boolean` | No       | `false` | This property will center the content horizontally. |

#### Modal.CloseButton props

The `<Modal.CloseButton>` component itself does not define any unique props, but any valid HTML
attributes of an
[HTMLButtonElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLButtonElement)
or a React `ref` will be passed onto the root `button` element that it renders. You will need to
use the `onClick` prop to close the modal.

#### Modal.Footer props

The `<Modal.Footer>` component itself does not define any unique props, but any valid HTML
attributes of an
[HTMLDivElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLDivElement)
or a React `ref` will be passed onto the root `div` element that it renders.

#### Modal.Header props

he `<Modal.Header>` component itself does not define any unique props, but any valid HTML
attributes of an
[HTMLDivElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLDivElement)
or a React `ref` will be passed onto the root `div` element that it renders.

### Custom hooks

#### useModal

The `useModal` hook is used to power the visibility mechanism between the
`Modal` and the button that triggers the visibility.

See the [Usage](#usage) section to learn how to use this hook.

##### Arguments

- `defaultIsOpen`: sets the default visibility of the component. `false` by default.

##### Return value

It provides props to be passed to each of these components:

- `triggerProps`: props to be passed to the element that triggers visibility, typically a button
- `modalProps`: props to be passed to the `Modal` component
- `state`: object containing the visibility state of the component, including functions to change the state
- `showModal`: boolean flag indicating if the `Modal` should be visible
- `close`: function that switches the visibility off
- `open`: function that switches the visibility on
- The `state` object contains other elements that are internal requirements for the `Modal` component

## Design

<FigmaLink href="https://www.figma.com/design/pDDlKkiRPt9NA2JdA6eyeY/%F0%9F%8C%AF-Burrito-Design-System-UI-Kit?node-id=11967-2543">
  Modal component in UI Kit
</FigmaLink>

### UX guidelines

<Design of={ModalStories.Default} />

components-toast--docs.content:
import { Meta, Title, Canvas, Controls, Source } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks' import { Design } from '@storybook/addon-designs/blocks' import { A11yTestStatus, FigmaLink } from '../../storybook/components' import * as ToastStories from './Toast.stories.tsx' import { sourceTransform } from './Toast.stories.utils.ts' Toast **Toasts** are used to display a short, transient message to users to provide feedback about an operation or action in a non-intrusive manner. They typically disappear after a brief period or upon user interaction. ## Usage

ℹ️ Note: In the Jane Admin app, the Toast is setup to react to messages that are dispatched via Redux, like dispatch(notificationActions.add(messageObj)).

Multiple Toasters on the same page will conflict with each other and can't be shown inline here. ### Tones The Burrito **Toast** comes in three tones: `success`, `critical`, and `info`. View the [**Tones** story](?path=/docs/components-toast--tones). ### Is Dismissible The **Toast** message will be displayed on mount with a "close" button and will remain visible until the user closes it. View the [**Is Dismissible** story](?path=/docs/components-toast--is-dismissible). ```tsx // Feature.tsx import { showToast } from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system' export const MyFeature = () => { React.useEffect(() => { showToast({ content: 'Message', timeout: false, isDismissible: true, }) }, []) return
} ``` ### Timeout Toasts normally disappear after short period of time which can be specified by the `timeout` parameter. View the [**Timeout** story](?path=/docs/components-toast--timeout). ### Multi-line Toast messages may span multiple lines of text. They can also contain links, but this is not recommended as it creates accessibility challenges. View the [**Multi-line** story](?path=/docs/components-toast--multi-line). ### Programmatic Dismissal If a Toast becomes irrelevant or redundant, it can be dismissed programmatically. View the [**Programmatic Dismissal** story](?path=/docs/components-toast--programmatic-dismissal). ### Custom z-index If the `Toasts` are hidden behind another element it means that you will need to adjust the `z-index` value of the `Toaster`. To achieve this you can use the `zIndex` prop. View the [**Custom z-index** story](?path=/docs/components-toast--custom-z-index). ### Show Toast outside a React component The following Redux thunk example will display a `success` style Toast when the API request returns successfully, and a `critical` style Toast when the API request throws an error. ```ts // reduxSlice.ts import { createAsyncThunk } from '@reduxjs/toolkit' import { showToast } from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system' const myThunk = createAsyncThunk('myThunk', async () => { try { const response = await someApi.fetch() showToast({ content: 'Success message', isDismissible: true, }) return response.data } catch (error) { showToast({ content: `Error message: ${error}`, tone: 'critical', timeout: false, isDismissible: true, }) } }) ``` ### Include `` in your application The Toaster establishes a region in the UI for **Toast** messages to be displayed at all times. It can only be included once in your application – multiple Toasters on the same page will share the same state and conflict with each other. A maximum of THREE Toast notifications can be displayed at the same time to avoid overwhelming the user. If more Toasts are submitted via `showToast()` then the oldest ones will be temporarily hidden until some visible Toasts have been dismissed. ```tsx // App.tsx import { Toaster } from '@janeapp/burrito-design-system' export const MyApp = () => { React.useEffect(() => closeAll, []) return ( <> ) } ``` ## Accessibility Most applicable accessibility requirements are built into the Toast and Toaster components. For example, hovering or focussing on the Toast will pause the auto-dismiss timer ([2.2.1 Timing Adjustable](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/timing-adjustable.html)). ### Allow enough time It is important to ensure that the Toast has a long enough `timeout` to allow the user to read and understand the entire message. For more than 15 words, Toasts should have a `timeout` of at least 10 seconds. Additionally, Toasts with critical actions should have no auto-dismissal (`timeout: false`) and ideally the actions will be included on the page where they will persist. ### Accessibility tests status #### General tests | Test | WCAG Level | Status | | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | | Color alone is not used to convey meaning | **A** ([1.4.1 Use of Color](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/use-of-color.html)) | | | Toasts meet color contrast requirements | **A** ([1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum)](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/contrast-minimum.html)) | | | Toasts remain functional when the screen is magnified to 400% of its original size | **A** ([1.4.10 Reflow](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/reflow.html)) | | | Enough time is provided to read the Toast message | **A** ([2.2.1 Timing Adjustable](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/timing-adjustable.html)) | | #### Keyboard navigation test While keyboard navigation _is_ supported, the shortcut key (F6) to jump to the region landmark where Toasts are displayed (where the Toaster is rendered) is not very discoverable. | Test | WCAG Level | Status | | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | | Toasts can be accessed with only a keyboard | **A** ([2.1.1 Keyboard](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/keyboard.html)) | | | When you use a keyboard to navigate the Toast, a focus indicator is present | **AA** ([2.4.7 Focus Visible](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/focus-visible.html)) | | #### Screen reader tests | Test | WCAG Level | Status | | ---------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- | | Screen reader announces Toast content | **AA** ([4.1.3 Status Messages](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/status-messages.html)) | | | Toast content is read in a logical order | **A** ([1.3.2 Meaningful sequence](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/Understanding/meaningful-sequence)) | | ## API ### Props Burrito implements a `` component, but does not provide it for use directly. Instead, **Toasts** are rendered by the `` component and toast are added through the `showToast` function. #### Toaster props The **Toaster** component supports valid HTML attributes of an [HTMLDivElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLDivElement) and will forward a React `ref` to its root `div` element, if provided. It also has the following additional props | Prop | Type | required | default | Description | | ------------ | -------- | -------- | ------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | **`zIndex`** | `number` | No | 1000 | The CSS `z-index` value for the Toaster and, by extension, the Toast messages themselves. | ### Supporting methods #### `showToast()` A method to add a Toast notification to the Toaster. Note that a maximum of 3 Toasts can be visible at the same time. ##### Parameters `showToast()` accepts a single argument, an object with the following properties: | Property | Type | Required | Default | Description | | --------------- | --------------------------------- | -------- | --------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `content` | `ReactNode` | No | None | The message of the Toast | | `tone` | `success` \| `critical` \| `info` | No | `success` | The visual style of the Toast | | `isDismissable` | `boolean` | No | `false` | Whether there is a close button that allows the user to dismiss the Toast manually. | | `timeout` | `boolean` \| `number` | No | `true` | Whether the Toast will dismiss after a specified delay.
true: Use default timeout (6 seconds)
false: No timeout (stays until manually dismissed)
number: Custom timeout in milliseconds (will be clamped between 6000 and 30000 ms) | | `onDismiss` | `function` | No | None | A callback to invoke after the Toast is dismissed: `() => void` | ##### Return value - `key` (`string`): identifier for this Toast object in the internal state #### `closeToast()` A function to dismiss the Toast with the provided `key` property. ##### Parameters `closeToast()` accepts a single argument: - `key` (`string`): identifier for the desired Toast to dismiss ##### Return value - None #### `closeAll()` A method to close all active Toasts by resetting the internal state. ##### Parameters - None ##### Return value - None ## Design Toast component in UI Kit ### UX guidelines

To take a trivial example, which of us ever undertakes laborious physical exercise, except to obtain some advantage from it? But who has any right to find fault with a man who chooses to enjoy a pleasure that has no annoying consequences, or one who avoids a pain that produces no resultant pleasure? components-tooltip--docs.content:
import { Meta, Title, Canvas } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'
import { Design } from '@storybook/addon-designs/blocks'
import { A11yTestStatus, FigmaLink } from '../../storybook/components'
import * as TooltipStories from './Tooltip.stories.tsx'

<Meta of={TooltipStories} />

<Title>Tooltip</Title>

**Tooltips** are used to show contextual help or information about specific
components when a user hovers or focuses on them.

## Usage

### Default

For the `Tooltip` component to work it needs to be used with `useTooltip` and an
element to point the tooltip to.

<Canvas of={TooltipStories.Default} sourceState="none" />

### Responsive sizing

The size of the tooltip will automatically adjust to its content and wrap when the content is too long.

<Canvas of={TooltipStories.ShortContent} />

### Custom z-index

If the `Tooltip` is hidden behind another element it means that you will need
to adjust the `z-index` value. To achieve this you can use the custom `zIndex`
prop.

<Canvas of={TooltipStories.CustomZIndex} />

### Automatic fallback position

By default, the `Tooltip` is positioned above its target but if there is not
enough space to be rendered there it will render somewhere else.

<Canvas of={TooltipStories.OnAPage} />

## Accessibility

### Using `Tooltip` with non-interactive elements

For accessibility reasons, `Tooltip` needs to be used on an element that is both
focusable and hoverable. By default, all interactive elements meet those
conditions (buttons, inputs...). But you may need to add a `Tooltip` on a
non-interactive elements, like an icon.

To enable keyboard navigation and proper cursor interaction, `useTooltip` makes
those elements focusable and hoverable by using `tabIndex=0`. However, you will
need to ensure that focus on that element becomes visible to meet criteria
[2.4.7 Focus visible](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/focus-visible). To achieve a Burrito focus style you can use the
[focus mixin](https://burrito-design-system.jane.qa/?path=/docs/foundations-mixins--docs)

### Using `Tooltip` with an icon

When using the `Tooltip` on an icon you will need to ensure that this icon has
an accessible label:

<Canvas of={TooltipStories.OnAnIcon} />

### Accessibility tests status

{/* Get accessibility tests from the USWDS https://designsystem.digital.gov/components/overview/ -- */}

#### General tests

| Test                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | WCAG Level                                                                                                         | Status                                          |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------- |
| Color is not the only method used to communicate the purpose of the tooltip. When you view the tooltip, the text, and never color alone, convey its purpose.                                                                                                    | **A** ([1.4.1 Use of color](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/use-of-color))                             | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest/> |
| Appropriate color contrast between tooltip text and background. When you view the tooltip and use ANDI or color contrast analyzer to look at the hex colors, contrast between the background and text is at least 4:5:1.                                        | **AA** ([1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum)](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/contrast-minimum))                  | <A11yTestStatus status="success"/>              |
| Appropriate color contrast between tooltip and page background. When you view the tooltip and use ANDI or color contrast analyzer to look at the hex colors, contrast between the tooltip component itself and any adjacent background content is at least 3:1. | **AA** ([1.4.11 Non-text contrast](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/non-text-contrast))                 | <A11yTestStatus status="conditional" />         |
| You can hover over tooltip content. When you move the pointer over an active tooltip, the tooltip stays open.                                                                                                                                                   | **AA** ([1.4.13 Content on hover or focus](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/content-on-hover-or-focus)) | <A11yTestStatus status="success"/>              |
| Active tooltips can be dismissed without moving the mouse or keyboard focus away from the tooltip trigger. When you press the escape key while the tooltip is active, the tooltip closes.                                                                       | **AA** ([1.4.13 Content on hover or focus](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/content-on-hover-or-focus)) | <A11yTestStatus status="success"/>              |
| Active tooltips stay open until they are dismissed. Active tooltips close only when you move focus away from the tooltip trigger or press the escape key.                                                                                                       | **AA** ([1.4.13 Content on hover or focus](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/content-on-hover-or-focus)) | <A11yTestStatus status="success"/>              |

#### Zoom navigation test

| Test                                                                                                                                                           | WCAG Level                                                                            | Status                                          |
| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| Using zoom does not obstruct the tooltip or other content. When you zoom to 200%, the tooltip content remains visible and does not overlap with other content. | **AA** ([1.4.4 Resize text](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/resize-text)) | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest/> |
| Tooltip remains visible and functional when screen is magnified. When you zoom to 200%, you can still read content in the tooltip and surrounding area easily. | **AA** ([1.4.10 Reflow](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/reflow))          | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest/> |

#### Keyboard navigation test

| Test                                                                                                                                                                               | WCAG Level                                                                                                                                  | Status                                  |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- |
| Tooltips can be accessed with only a keyboard. When you tab to an element that has a tooltip, the tooltip activates.                                                               | **A** ([2.1.1 Keyboard](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/keyboard))                                                              | <A11yTestStatus status="success"/>      |
| The tooltip does not trap the user or move the user around the page unwillingly. When you open a tooltip, you can navigate in and out of the trigger component without difficulty. | **A** ([2.1.2 No keyboard trap](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/no-keyboard-trap://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/reflow)) | <A11yTestStatus status="success"/>      |
| Focus indicator is clearly visible around trigger element. When you tab into an element with a tooltip, a visible focus indicator appears around that element.                     | **AA** ([2.4.7 Focus visible](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/focus-visible))                                                   | <A11yTestStatus status="conditional" /> |

#### Screen reader tests

| Test                                                                                                                                                                                                             | WCAG Level                                                                                                 | Status                                          |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| Screen reader announces tooltip content. When you focus on the tooltip trigger, the screen reader announces the tooltip content.                                                                                 | **A** ([1.3.1 Info and relationships](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/info-and-relationships)) | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest/> |
| Tooltip content is read in a logical order. When you listen to the tooltip with a screen reader, you first hear the trigger element content, then the tooltip content, followed by the next content on the page. | **A** ([1.3.2 Meaningful sequence](https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG21/Understanding/meaningful-sequence))       | <A11yTestStatus status="success" isManualTest/> |

## API

### Props

The Tooltip component has all the props present on a [HTMLDivElement](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLDivElement)
and requires additional props that will be provided by the `useTooltip` hook:

| Prop           | Type     | required | default | Description                                                                                      |
| -------------- | -------- | -------- | ------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **`state`**    | `object` | Yes      | N/A     | This object describes the current state of the tooltip: visibility, position, arrow position     |
| **`arrowRef`** | `ref`    | Yes      | N/A     | The `ref` object for the Tooltip arrow. It's a requirement for the `useTooltip` to work properly |
| **`zIndex`**   | `number` | No       | 100     | The CSS `z-index` value for the Tooltip                                                          |

### Custom hooks

### useTooltip

The `useTooltip` hook is used to power the visibility and position mechanism
between the `Tooltip` and its target element.

See the [Usage](#usage) section to learn how to use this hook

#### Arguments

`useTooltip` takes an object as argument. This object can have the following
fields:

- `defaultIsOpen`: sets the default visibility of the component. `false` by default
- `triggerRef`: Optional fields that you can use in case you have need to pass
  your own `ref` to the trigger element
- `tooltipRef`: Optional fields that you can use in case you have need to pass
  your own `ref` to the `Tooltip`
- `triggerProps`: Optional object containing DOM attributes and event handlers that you can use in case you need to compose custom event handlers (like `onFocus`, `onClick`, etc.) in addition to the Tooltip's built-in behavior.

#### Return value

`useTooltip` provides props to be passed on the `Tooltip` component and its
targeting element:

- `triggerProps`: props to be passed to the element that open the `Tooltip`, typically a button
- `tooltipProps`: props to be passed to the `Tooltip` component

## Design

  Tooltip component in UI Kit
</FigmaLink>
